definition: Central bank balance sheets will not return to pre-GFC levels due to structural drivers: floor-system monetary implementation requires ample reserves, LCR/NSFR creates inelastic HQLA demand, payment system volumes have roughly doubled since 2008, and NBFI growth requires larger backstop facilities. The Fed's reserve floor estimate is ~$3-3.5T vs ~$15-45B pre-GFC.
evidence: Floor-system monetary implementation requires ample reserves
Payment system volumes roughly doubled since 2008
Ratchet effect: 0% historical base rate for full normalization across 6 episodes (generalist_02)
QT2 has reduced balance sheet by 25% over 45 months but not to pre-crisis ratios
open_questions: What is the new-normal equilibrium balance sheet size?
Does the ratchet effect imply balance sheets will grow with each crisis cycle indefinitely?
